description_long: This kiosk features a robust, square structure with four thick, marble-textured pillars supporting a large, hipped roof made of reddish-brown stone or tile. The roof has a prominent central cap and wide overhangs, providing ample shelter beneath. The lower half of the kiosk is partially enclosed with horizontal wooden slats, creating a semi-open barrier on three sides while leaving one side more accessible. The interior is open and airy, allowing for easy movement or service from within. The materials and design suggest durability and weather resistance, suitable for outdoor urban environments such as parks or plazas. The overall aesthetic is clean and functional, with a hint of traditional architectural influence.
description: A sturdy, marble-pillared kiosk with a reddish-brown hipped roof and partial wooden slat enclosures, designed for outdoor urban use. The structure is open and inviting, blending traditional and functional elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing two marble pillars, the open entryway, and the overhanging hipped roof with a central cap.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the wooden slat barrier and the depth of the roof overhang, with two pillars visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full extent of the wooden slat enclosure and the symmetry of the roof and pillars.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the wooden slats and the marble pillars, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
attribute_roof_design: Hipped roof with a central cap, made of reddish-brown stone or tile, wide overhangs, no skylights or decorative elements.
attribute_architectural_style: Traditional with utilitarian influences, featuring classical marble pillars and a simple hipped roof.
